title: Scanline — a software 3D renderer
description: A real-time software 3D renderer built from scratch in TypeScript — no WebGL, no GPU. Every triangle is transformed, near-plane clipped in homogeneous space (Sutherland–Hodgman), perspective-divided and scan-converted by an edge-function rasterizer with perspective-correct barycentric interpolation, a z-buffer and backface culling. The fragment stage now offers TWO lighting models: classic Blinn–Phong and a metallic-roughness Cook–Torrance PBR path (GGX NDF, Smith geometry, Fresnel–Schlick) lit by a fully analytic HDR environment that drives a real skybox, diffuse irradiance ambient and roughness-blurred specular reflections. Tangent-space normal mapping perturbs the shading normal from procedural maps; a Float32 HDR buffer feeds a post pipeline with bloom, ACES/Reinhard/Filmic tone mapping, FXAA and vignette. Real-time PCF shadow mapping from the key light. Paste-import any Wavefront OBJ (auto-normals, auto-fit) and export the frame to PNG. Eight debug views (shaded, albedo, wireframe, depth, normals, UV, overdraw heatmap, near-clip) make the whole pipeline legible. Orbit camera, six scenes incl. a PBR metalness×roughness sweep, live FPS/triangle/fill stats.
